blocks.content:
Getting observability data from your applications typically requires manual instrumentation — adding SDKs, updating code to capture spans, and deploying instrumented services. For a team with dozens of services, full instrumentation coverage can take weeks or months.

Instrumentation Hub flips this model by providing automatic discovery and zero-code instrumentation for your Kubernetes services.

With Instrumentation Hub, you can:

- **Discover services automatically** — Your services are found when Alloy is installed, no registration needed
- **Instrument without code changes** — Beyla (eBPF) captures telemetry at the kernel level
- **Get data in minutes** — Enable instrumentation and see RED metrics within minutes
- **Support multiple languages** — Works across Go, Java, Python, Node.js, and more

After instrumenting a service, you'll have RED metrics (request rate, error rate, and duration), service-to-service links showing how services communicate, and optionally distributed traces, application logs, and profiling.

In the next milestone, you'll access Instrumentation Hub and see your services discovered.

---

### More to explore (optional)

At this point in your journey, you can explore the following paths:

- [The RED method - How to instrument your services](https://grafana.com/blog/2018/08/02/the-red-method-how-to-instrument-your-services/)
- [Getting started with Beyla](https://grafana.com/docs/beyla/latest/tutorial/getting-started/)
- [Introduction to Application Observability](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/monitor-applications/application-observability/)
